what_changed: Iterator identity adapter contracts now expose fixed result-domain evidence for safe rows: Rust iter/into_iter/iter_mut/copied/cloned and Java stream produce Iterator, while Rust to_vec produces Collection.
Rust collect remains closed for fixed result-domain emission because its result type is caller-selected.
Strict exact receiver-domain gates now consume asserted Domain evidence anchored to exact receiver nodes, including Call receivers, instead of restricting the cache facade to Var receivers.
Rust typed const/static items and typed let bindings now emit binding-domain evidence through the existing binding-domain resolver.

Collection literal assignments now materialize dependency-backed binding-domain evidence from the literal SequenceSurface/Domain proof, including local Rust/Swift bindings and JS module-scope bindings.

Canonical builtin admission can validate binding-domain proof chains after normalization inlines the receiver value, including static/module map bindings, while strict exact keeps mutation-sensitive receivers closed when ReceiverMutation evidence appears before use.
Receiver-domain lookup for alpha-renamed method receivers can follow a captured enclosing function parameter domain inside a lambda without crossing function boundaries.

notes: The selected bucket moved 241 -> 239 and completeness improved 38/82 -> 39/83 by letting normalized receiver values retain dependency-backed binding-domain proof instead of selector-specific exceptions.

The total exact-admission rejection count increased by 1 while hard gates stayed closed; the increase is attributed to structured callee-identity/HOF/library-API occurrence buckets in newly touched code, not to unattributed exact-safety loss.

Strict exact now rejects mutation-sensitive receiver-domain use when ReceiverMutation evidence appears before the receiver use, preserving the Swift Set(sequence) mutation boundary while still admitting unmutated binding-domain receivers.
decision.follow_up: Design cross-file field/constant domain provenance before trying to recover f.locations.iter(), imported generated constant arrays, or similar external receiver surfaces.
